what_it_does: An empty .gitkeep file that causes Git to track the otherwise empty company/fabric/ directory.
standouts: Its only role is to preserve an empty directory under version control
conventions: Follows the common Git convention of using a .gitkeep file to retain an empty directory in version control
Uses a leading-dot filename for repository housekeeping
notes: An empty Git placeholder that keeps the company/fabric/ directory tracked.
questions: What files or subdirectories are expected to be added to fabric/ later?
purpose_vs_actual: The implied purpose (preserve the directory in Git) matches the empty .gitkeep content.
intention_signals: Reserve the fabric/ directory as a tracked repository location, likely in anticipation of future content or as a structural boundary.
contribution: It preserves the existence and path of the fabric/ directory in version-control history, ensuring any tooling or documentation referencing company/fabric/ sees a tracked location.
summary_for_embedding: An empty .gitkeep file inside company/fabric/ that causes Git to track the otherwise empty directory.
intention_for_embedding: To maintain the fabric/ directory under version control until substantive files are added.
